title: Add missing fields to EventEnvelope
how_to_think: Think like an event contract architect. The invariant to protect is: every emitted event carries the full constitutional envelope metadata from day one. The failure mode to avoid is: permanent event streams missing versioning/tracing/reversal semantics that become migration traps.
acceptance_criteria: EventEnvelope<T> includes: event_type, schema_version, trace_id, reverses_event_id, supersedes_event_id, side_effect_id, replay_safe, mutation_class
Envelope compiles across all crates with no optional schema shortcuts

title: Fix envelope validation logic + unit tests
how_to_think: Think like a defensive validator engineer. The invariant to protect is: envelope validation checks the correct fields and rejects invalid metadata deterministically. The failure mode to avoid is: false passes causing silent non-compliant events.
acceptance_criteria: validate_envelope_fields() validates the correct field set (no duplicate checks / missing checks)
Unit tests prove invalid event_type, schema_version, trace_id, mutation_class are rejected

title: Add envelope metadata columns to AR outbox
how_to_think: Think like a database integrity specialist. The invariant to protect is: envelope metadata is stored as queryable columns in AR outbox. The failure mode to avoid is: metadata trapped in JSON or missing, breaking auditability and replay safety.
acceptance_criteria: AR outbox schema includes required envelope metadata columns (nullable or defaulted for backfill)
Migration applies cleanly to a real AR database
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p ar_rs

title: Add envelope metadata columns to Payments outbox
how_to_think: Think like a database integrity specialist. The invariant to protect is: envelope metadata is stored as queryable columns in Payments outbox. The failure mode to avoid is: unqueryable metadata that blocks governance and diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: Payments outbox schema includes required envelope metadata columns (nullable or defaulted for backfill)
Migration applies cleanly to a real Payments database
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p payments_rs

title: Add envelope metadata columns to Subscriptions outbox
how_to_think: Think like a database integrity specialist. The invariant to protect is: envelope metadata is stored as queryable columns in Subscriptions outbox. The failure mode to avoid is: missing envelope metadata preventing deterministic replay analysis.
acceptance_criteria: Subscriptions outbox schema includes required envelope metadata columns (nullable or defaulted for backfill)
Migration applies cleanly to a real Subscriptions database
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p subscriptions_rs

title: Add envelope metadata columns to GL outbox
how_to_think: Think like a database integrity specialist. The invariant to protect is: envelope metadata is stored as queryable columns in GL outbox. The failure mode to avoid is: inability to trace postings/reversals via envelope metadata.
acceptance_criteria: GL outbox schema includes required envelope metadata columns (nullable or defaulted for backfill)
Migration applies cleanly to a real GL database
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p gl_rs

title: Add envelope metadata columns to Notifications outbox
how_to_think: Think like a database integrity specialist. The invariant to protect is: envelope metadata is stored as queryable columns in Notifications outbox. The failure mode to avoid is: operational events that cannot be traced or versioned reliably.
acceptance_criteria: Notifications outbox schema includes required envelope metadata columns (nullable or defaulted for backfill)
Migration applies cleanly to a real Notifications database
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p notifications_rs

title: Require envelope validation at enqueue boundary
how_to_think: Think like a platform boundary enforcer. The invariant to protect is: no outbox insert occurs without passing envelope validation. The failure mode to avoid is: bypass paths emitting non-compliant events.
acceptance_criteria: Outbox enqueue helper rejects invalid envelopes before insert
All module emit sites route through the validated enqueue helper (no direct inserts)

title: Add E2E test: invalid envelope rejected
how_to_think: Think like a contract compliance tester. The invariant to protect is: invalid envelope metadata cannot be persisted or published. The failure mode to avoid is: corrupt event stream that survives CI.
acceptance_criteria: E2E test attempts to emit an event with invalid envelope metadata
Test asserts: request fails and outbox row is not created
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests envelope_invalid_rejected_e2e -- --nocapture

title: Add E2E envelope NATS roundtrip test
how_to_think: Think like a contract compliance tester. The invariant to protect is: envelope metadata survives publish-subscribe delivery unchanged. The failure mode to avoid is: metadata lost in transit or remapped incorrectly by publisher/consumer.
acceptance_criteria: E2E test publishes event and verifies all envelope fields survive NATS roundtrip
Test validates trace_id, schema_version, mutation_class, replay_safe are preserved
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests envelope_roundtrip_e2e -- --nocapture

title: Create mutation class registry + enum wiring
how_to_think: Think like a financial controls auditor. The invariant to protect is: every domain concept declares a mutation_class and every event carries it. The failure mode to avoid is: illegal updates to financial/audit records with no policy enforcement.
acceptance_criteria: docs/governance/MUTATION-CLASSES.md exists and defines classification rules
mutation_class is a required EventEnvelope field and cannot be None/empty at runtime

title: Classify core domain concepts in registry
how_to_think: Think like an accounting policy owner. The invariant to protect is: strict_immutable/compensating_required concepts are identified before enforcement. The failure mode to avoid is: retroactive policy edits after data exists.
acceptance_criteria: Registry classifies: invoices, payment_attempts, journal_entries, subscription cycles, reconciliation artifacts
Each concept lists owner module

title: Populate mutation_class at all emit sites
how_to_think: Think like a governance enforcement engineer. The invariant to protect is: every emitted event has the correct mutation_class matching the registry. The failure mode to avoid is: incorrect class tagging that undermines audit and reversal policy.
acceptance_criteria: All emit sites set mutation_class explicitly (no defaults)
Integrated test asserts at least one event per module carries the expected mutation_class
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests mutation_class_presence_e2e -- --nocapture

title: Wire reverses/supersedes into envelopes
how_to_think: Think like a replay-safety engineer. The invariant to protect is: reversal and supersession linkage is represented at the envelope level, enabling deterministic replay and projection correctness. The failure mode to avoid is: compensating events that cannot be linked during replay.
acceptance_criteria: Emit helpers support reverses_event_id and supersedes_event_id (nullable)
At least one real reversal flow emits envelope metadata correctly
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests envelope_reversal_linkage_e2e -- --nocapture

title: Enforce max reversal chain depth = 1
how_to_think: Think like a ledger correctness auditor. The invariant to protect is: reversal/supersession chains never exceed depth 1 as required. The failure mode to avoid is: recursive reversals that make projections ambiguous.
acceptance_criteria: Invariant rejects attempts to reverse a reversal or supersede a supersession
Integrated test demonstrates rejection behavior
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests reversal_chain_depth_enforcement_e2e -- --nocapture

title: Fix AR outbox atomicity + prove via E2E
how_to_think: Think like an outbox correctness engineer. The invariant to protect is: domain state changes and outbox inserts commit atomically in one transaction. The failure mode to avoid is: state mutation with missing events (silent inconsistency).
acceptance_criteria: AR invoice mutation + outbox insert occur in a single SQL transaction
E2E test proves no state change can persist without an outbox row
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests ar_outbox_atomicity_e2e -- --nocapture

title: Audit Payments outbox atomicity + prove via E2E
how_to_think: Think like a payment integrity engineer. The invariant to protect is: payment lifecycle mutations and emitted events are atomic. The failure mode to avoid is: UNKNOWN/RETRY transitions that never publish events.
acceptance_criteria: Representative payment transition is transactionally atomic with outbox insert
E2E test proves atomicity under failure injection
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests payments_outbox_atomicity_e2e -- --nocapture

title: Audit Subscriptions outbox atomicity + prove via E2E
how_to_think: Think like a billing correctness engineer. The invariant to protect is: subscription cycle mutations and outbox events are atomic. The failure mode to avoid is: cycle advance without invoice intent emission.
acceptance_criteria: Representative subscription cycle mutation is transactionally atomic with outbox insert
E2E test proves atomicity
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests subscriptions_outbox_atomicity_e2e -- --nocapture

title: Audit GL outbox atomicity + prove via E2E
how_to_think: Think like a ledger event integrity engineer. The invariant to protect is: GL posting state changes and emitted events are atomic. The failure mode to avoid is: postings without events or events without postings.
acceptance_criteria: Representative GL mutation+outbox is atomic
E2E test proves atomicity
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests gl_outbox_atomicity_e2e -- --nocapture

title: Audit Notifications outbox atomicity + prove via E2E
how_to_think: Think like an event delivery integrity specialist. The invariant to protect is: notification state changes and outbox events are atomic. The failure mode to avoid is: notification state drifting from published events.
acceptance_criteria: Representative notification transition is atomic with outbox insert
E2E test proves atomicity
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests notifications_outbox_atomicity_e2e -- --nocapture

title: Centralize DB pool resolver in AR
how_to_think: Think like an isolation-tier architect. The invariant to protect is: AR creates DB pools only via a single resolver seam. The failure mode to avoid is: scattered pool creation blocking future tenant routing/PDAA.
acceptance_criteria: AR main.rs uses db::resolve_pool() only (no inline PgPoolOptions::new().connect())
Resolver is the only PgPool construction point

title: Centralize DB pool resolver in Payments
how_to_think: Think like a platform seam enforcer. The invariant to protect is: Payments creates DB pools only via resolver. The failure mode to avoid is: inconsistent connection behavior blocking PDAA adoption.
acceptance_criteria: Payments main.rs uses resolver only
No inline pool creation remains

title: Centralize DB pool resolver in Subscriptions
how_to_think: Think like a governance engineer. The invariant to protect is: Subscriptions creates DB pools only via resolver. The failure mode to avoid is: config drift and operational inconsistency.
acceptance_criteria: Subscriptions main.rs uses resolver only
No inline pool creation remains

title: Centralize DB pool resolver in Notifications
how_to_think: Think like a platform hardening engineer. The invariant to protect is: Notifications creates DB pools only via resolver. The failure mode to avoid is: future isolation migration requiring multi-file rewrites.
acceptance_criteria: Notifications main.rs uses resolver only
No inline pool creation remains

title: Add lint: forbid cross-module imports
how_to_think: Think like a CI guardrail engineer. The invariant to protect is: no cross-module source imports ever land. The failure mode to avoid is: accidental coupling that destroys modularity.
acceptance_criteria: Lint script detects cross-module imports and fails with actionable output
CI runs lint on PRs
verification: Script exits 0 on clean repo; CI wired

title: Add lint: forbid raw PgPool creation
how_to_think: Think like a platform seam enforcer. The invariant to protect is: DB pools are only created via resolver. The failure mode to avoid is: bypassing the one-way door enabling future isolation tiers.
acceptance_criteria: Lint fails if PgPoolOptions::new().connect() appears outside db/resolver.rs per module
CI runs lint and blocks violations
verification: Script exits 0 on clean repo; CI wired

title: Add lint: require event metadata present
how_to_think: Think like an event governance reviewer. The invariant to protect is: every emitted event has required metadata populated. The failure mode to avoid is: silent contract drift that breaks consumers.
acceptance_criteria: Script fails if emit sites omit event_type, schema_version, trace_id, mutation_class
CI runs script and blocks violations
verification: Script exits 0 on clean repo; CI wired

title: Add /ready endpoints (DB reachable)
how_to_think: Think like an SRE. The invariant to protect is: readiness implies DB connectivity and critical init complete. The failure mode to avoid is: routing traffic to a live process that cannot access its database.
acceptance_criteria: All modules expose /ready and it checks DB connectivity
E2E smoke test confirms /ready returns 200 only when DB up
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests ready_endpoints_smoke_e2e -- --nocapture

title: Add /version endpoints (name+ver+schema)
how_to_think: Think like an ops diagnostician. The invariant to protect is: every module can report exact build and schema version. The failure mode to avoid is: blind debugging during deploys and migrations.
acceptance_criteria: All modules expose /version returning module name, module version, schema version
E2E test validates payload shape across modules
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests version_endpoints_smoke_e2e -- --nocapture

title: Create backup script + runbook baseline
how_to_think: Think like a disaster recovery engineer. The invariant to protect is: daily backups exist and restore is documented and repeatable. The failure mode to avoid is: total data loss with no recovery path.
acceptance_criteria: Repo contains a daily backup script for all module DBs
Runbook documents a restore procedure (single-tenant restore minimum)
verification: Backup artifacts created successfully for each DB

title: Add quarterly restore verification test
how_to_think: Think like a recovery validation specialist. The invariant to protect is: backups are provably restorable. The failure mode to avoid is: backups that silently fail until the day you need them.
acceptance_criteria: Restore verification script restores one module DB to a scratch DB and runs an integrity query
CI job (manual trigger acceptable) runs restore verification
verification: Restore completes and integrity query succeeds

title: Add domain ownership registry
how_to_think: Think like a platform governance lead. The invariant to protect is: each domain concept has exactly one owning module declared. The failure mode to avoid is: overlapping ownership forcing cross-module coupling and inconsistent truth.
acceptance_criteria: Domain ownership registry exists and lists owners for AR/GL/Payments/Subscriptions/Notifications
Registry includes inter-module command list with declared write degradation class

title: Declare degradation class for Sub→AR
how_to_think: Think like a resilience engineer. The invariant to protect is: every inter-module write command has an explicit degradation class and behavior. The failure mode to avoid is: implicit retries/hangs under dependency outage.
acceptance_criteria: Subscriptions→AR command documented with degradation class + timeout budget
Implementation contains no implicit retry beyond declared policy

title: Add degradation E2E: Sub→AR target down
how_to_think: Think like an integration test engineer. The invariant to protect is: declared degradation behavior is proven under target unavailability. The failure mode to avoid is: production outage causing deadlocks or inconsistent state.
acceptance_criteria: E2E test simulates AR unavailable and asserts declared degradation outcome
Test asserts no crash/hang and no undeclared retry behavior
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests subscriptions_ar_degradation_e2e -- --nocapture
Test passes and matches declared degradation class

title: Add retention class declarations doc
how_to_think: Think like a data lifecycle owner. The invariant to protect is: every domain concept category has a declared retention class. The failure mode to avoid is: uncontrolled storage growth and unclear retention posture.
acceptance_criteria: Retention classes declared for permanent/long/medium/short per module concept categories
Doc aligns with constitution definitions

title: Add correlation ID to AR invoicing
how_to_think: Think like a distributed tracing engineer. The invariant to protect is: invoices can be correlated across lifecycle boundaries. The failure mode to avoid is: untraceable invoice creation despite trace_id presence.
acceptance_criteria: AR invoices table has correlation_id column
Invoice creation captures correlation_id from upstream context
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p ar_rs

title: Add correlation ID to payment attempts
how_to_think: Think like a payment flow tracer. The invariant to protect is: payment attempts propagate correlation_id from invoice. The failure mode to avoid is: broken trace chain at payment boundary.
acceptance_criteria: payment_attempts table has correlation_id
Payment attempt creation propagates correlation_id from invoice
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p payments_rs

title: Add correlation ID to GL posting
how_to_think: Think like a financial audit tracer. The invariant to protect is: GL entries inherit correlation_id from originating AR/Payment context. The failure mode to avoid is: GL entries with no trail to source transactions.
acceptance_criteria: journal_entries table has correlation_id
GL posting propagates correlation_id from source context
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p gl_rs

title: Add Prometheus metrics to AR
how_to_think: Think like an SRE. The invariant to protect is: AR exposes operational metrics without breaking request paths. The failure mode to avoid is: metrics code that destabilizes hot paths or hides failures.
acceptance_criteria: AR exposes /metrics endpoint
Metrics include: invoices_created_total, invoices_paid_total, invoice_age_seconds
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p ar_rs

title: Add Prometheus metrics to Payments
how_to_think: Think like a payment ops monitor. The invariant to protect is: payment outcomes are metered accurately. The failure mode to avoid is: misleading metrics or missing UNKNOWN visibility.
acceptance_criteria: Payments exposes /metrics
Metrics include: payment_attempts_total{status}, unknown_duration_seconds, retry_attempts_total
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p payments_rs

title: Add Prometheus metrics to Subscriptions
how_to_think: Think like a billing cycle observer. The invariant to protect is: billing cycles are instrumented without changing behavior. The failure mode to avoid is: metrics introducing side effects.
acceptance_criteria: Subscriptions exposes /metrics
Metrics include: cycles_attempted_total, cycles_completed_total, subscription_churn_total
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p subscriptions_rs

title: Add Prometheus metrics to GL
how_to_think: Think like a financial operations monitor. The invariant to protect is: GL posting activity is observable without affecting correctness. The failure mode to avoid is: metrics that mask posting errors or miscount entries.
acceptance_criteria: GL exposes /metrics
Metrics include: journal_entries_total, posting_errors_total
verification: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p gl_rs

title: Define alert thresholds doc
how_to_think: Think like an incident response architect. The invariant to protect is: alerts are tied to explicit SLO/threshold definitions. The failure mode to avoid is: arbitrary alerting that trains teams to ignore pages.
acceptance_criteria: Alert thresholds doc defines UNKNOWN duration, retry exhaustion, invariant failure criteria
Thresholds include warning/critical levels

title: Add UNKNOWN duration alert rule
how_to_think: Think like a payment reliability guardian. The invariant to protect is: UNKNOWN states are surfaced before customer impact. The failure mode to avoid is: silent payment limbo with no operational visibility.
acceptance_criteria: Prometheus alert rule exists for UNKNOWN duration > documented threshold
Rule is validated by promtool

title: Add invariant failure alert rule
how_to_think: Think like a data integrity sentinel. The invariant to protect is: invariant failures page immediately. The failure mode to avoid is: silent corruption.
acceptance_criteria: Prometheus alert rule exists for invariant failure metric > 0
Rule is validated by promtool
